title: Sha Koon
content:
{{Top|ffa|leg}} {{Character |type=Jedi |image=[[File:ShaKoonHSSTD.png]] |name=Sha Koon |homeworld=[[Dorin/Legends|Dorin]]''[[Purge – Seconds to Die]]'' |birth= |death=[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] |species=[[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]][[Star Wars (1998) 39|''Star Wars'' (1998) 39]] |gender=[[Gender/Legends|Female]] |pronouns=She/her |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Black]] with [[Color/Legends|silver]] irises |skin=[[Color/Legends|Orange]] |cyber= |families=[[Koon family]] |parents=new |partners=new |siblings=new |children=new |affiliation=*[[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] *[[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] |masters=[[Saldith]] {{C|[[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]}} |apprentices=[[Baylis Archan]] {{C|[[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]}} }} {{Quote|She died at ''peace''? Not despair? Are you certain of this, Lord Vader?"''
"''I am sure of what I felt, my Lord.|Emperor Palpatine and Darth Vader, referring to Sha Koon|}} '''Sha Koon''' was a [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and the [[Koon family|niece]] of [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]]. Her uncle, who shared a [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] bond with her, brought Koon from her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Dorin/Legends|Dorin]] to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] to begin her initial [[Jedi training/Legends|training]] under [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. Koon eventually became the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] Master [[Saldith]]. In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], she was involved in the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Stark Commercial Combine]] and assisted her uncle and the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] in bringing an end to the conflict. After rising to the rank of Jedi Knight, Koon took the Jedi [[Baylis Archan]] as her Padawan. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], the final year of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Koon fought alongside Jedi Knight [[Halagad Ventor]] and a detachment of Republic [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in a [[Battle of Bal'demnic|battle]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic]]. Later that year, Koon, Archan, and a command of [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] fought against the Separatists in a [[Battle of Giju (Clone Wars)|battle]] on [[Giju/Legends|Giju]]. During that engagement, Koon's [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] were killed, along with her Padawan. After the battle, Koon returned to the Jedi Temple and refused to resume fighting in the war. Shortly after the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku's]] death during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], Koon was tasked by her uncle to search the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] for any information regarding the mysterious [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] that Dooku had mentioned to be in control of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. While at the Archives, Koon was targeted as part of the initial stages of [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], which was [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|order]] to the Republic's [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] troopers to eliminate their [[Jedi General/Legends|Jedi Generals]]. She escaped the [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|attack]] on the Jedi Temple, which was led by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Weeks after Palpatine [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], Koon lured [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader into a trap in the Jedi Temple's underlevels. In the [[Duel in the Jedi Temple Underlevels|ensuing duel]], Koon was able to injure Vader but was mortally wounded by the Sith Lord. Before she died, Koon experienced a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] of Vader's redemption, Palpatine's [[Battle of Endor/Legends|death]], and the [[New Jedi Order|return]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] through Vader's children, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. ==Biography== ===Early life and Jedi training=== {{Quote|Saldith said it was urgent you speak with us. More urgent than sleeping?''"
"''Yes, Masters. My uncle wants much to communicate with you.|Mace Windu and Sha Koon|}} [[File:ShaKoon.jpg|thumb|left|120px|Sha Koon during the Stark Hyperspace War]] Sha Koon was a [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] [[Gender/Legends|female]] born into the [[Koon family]], which had a reputation for being strong with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], during the final [[Time/Legends|decades]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]. She possessed a [[Telepathy/Legends|telepathic]] bond with her uncle, [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] [[Plo Koon/Legends|Plo Koon]]. At some point during her [[Youngling/Legends|childhood]], her uncle came to her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of [[Dorin/Legends|Dorin]] to bring her to the [[Jedi Temple/Legends|Jedi Temple]] on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]]. Because of her bond with her uncle, the young Koon understood that he had come to take her to be [[Jedi training/Legends|trained]] as a Jedi without having to ask. She began her initial instruction under [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Grand Master/Legends|Grand Master]] [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]] and was eventually assigned to be the [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] of the [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Saldith]]. In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]] between the Galactic Republic and the [[Stark Commercial Combine]], Koon remained at the Jedi Temple. Her uncle Plo Koon, meanwhile, was on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]] in an attempt to resolve the conflict with Masters [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and the pair's respective Padawans, [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. Shortly after the [[Fourth Battle of Qotile]], Koon was contacted by her uncle through the telepathic bond that they shared. Plo Koon asked his niece to go to the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]] so that he could communicate with the Councilors through her. Koon and her Master, Saldith, met with the Council, and Koon informed the Masters of the Council that her uncle wanted to speak with them. Saldith reassured the Council that Koon's mind was calm and told the Jedi Council member [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] that he had felt it best to approach the Council on this matter, since Saldith knew that the Kel Dor species communicated with their minds. The Councilors joined their minds with Koon's, contacting Plo Koon and the Jedi on Troiken. After speaking with Plo Koon, the Council sent a Jedi strike force to assist his team, which ultimately helped to bring a resolution to the Stark [[Hyperspace/Legends|Hyperspace]] War. ===The Clone Wars=== {{Quote|They tell me you are recovered, Sha Koon."''
"''Physically, yes. Have you come to suggest it was time I returned to the war? I will tell you as I have told the others—I will not go back.|Plo and Sha Koon|}} After Koon passed the [[Jedi Trials/Legends|trials]] necessary to become a Jedi Knight, she took the [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Baylis Archan]]—who possessed telepathic abilities like she did—as her Padawan. In [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]],''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] between the Galactic Republic and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]], Koon was part of a task force sent by the Jedi Council to respond to intelligence that revealed the Separatists' intent to [[Mining/Legends|mine]] [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]]-resistant [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] [[Ore/Legends|ore]] on the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] world of [[Bal'demnic/Legends|Bal'demnic]]. The task force—which included Jedi Knight [[Halagad Ventor]], several other Jedi, and a back-up squad of [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]—was assigned to investigate and terminate the CIS operation.{{DB|location|baldemnic|Bal'demnic}} [[File:BattleOfGiju-STD.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Sha Koon and her troopers engage the Separatists on Giju.]] When Koon's forces arrived on Bal'demnic, they found the planet in chaos, with the native [[Kon'me]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Battle of Bal'demnic|rebelling against]] the Confederacy. After the [[Cloning/Legends|clone]] [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] landed, the Kon'me became furious and attacked all of the off-worlders. Although Koon and her forces were forced to retreat from Bal'demnic, the Confederacy also halted its mining operation on the planet. After the battle, the Republic assigned a [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] to protect Bal'demnic from further Separatist operations. Later that year, Koon and Baylis Archan were sent with a command of clones to the planet [[Giju/Legends|Giju]]. A [[Battle of Giju (Clone Wars)|battle]] ensued with the Separatists, and both Koon's forces and her Padawan were [[Death/Legends|killed]]. After the battle and Archan's demise, Koon returned to Coruscant to recover from her wounds, particularly one to her right eye, and to [[Meditation/Legends|meditate]] on her actions. She refused to return to the war. Shortly after the death of the Confederate [[Head of State/Legends|Head of State]] and [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]] during the [[Battle of Coruscant/Legends|Battle of Coruscant]], Koon was assigned by her uncle to search the [[Jedi Archives/Legends|Jedi Archives]] for any leads regarding the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] that Dooku had claimed was in control of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate]]. Koon met with [[Jedi archivist/Legends|Jedi librarian]] [[Jocasta Nu/Legends|Jocasta Nu]], who offered Koon her assistance. Koon refused, however, believing that the Jedi librarian would be unable to help her. As Koon was searching through the Archives, Plo Koon was [[Battle of Cato Neimoidia/Legends|murdered]] in [[Cato Neimoidia/Legends|Cato Neimoidia's]] [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] by his clone troopers when [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] issued [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]], a [[Contingency Orders for the Grand Army of the Republic: Order Initiation, Orders 1 Through 150|directive]] that instructed all Republic [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] to eliminate the Jedi.{{Film|III}} Koon [[Force sense/Legends|felt]] her uncle's death both through the Force and in her mind and lost consciousness. Koon awoke to find members of the [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army of the Republic's]] [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] [[Operation: Knightfall/Legends|attacking]] the Jedi Temple. The clones she ran into attacked her, and Koon deflected their [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] bolts with her lightsaber. While fleeing the soldiers, Koon witnessed Jedi Knight [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]]—who had recently been christened as the Sith Lord [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader—kill Jocasta Nu. Koon felt Skywalker's emotions and immediately realized that he had fallen to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and joined the Sith. Koon then fled from Vader and his [[Legion/Legends|legion]] by way of the secret passageways under the Jedi Temple that she had learned from Padawan [[Jin-Lo Rayce]]. ===Confronting Darth Vader=== {{Quote|Im…pressive. But…to kill me…you must now…''face'' me."''
"''Gladly. Killer of younglings. Betrayer of friends. Destroyer of the Jedi to whom you claimed allegiance. Animal. I have longed for this day."''
"''As have I. With your death…my mission is complete. You…are the last…|Darth Vader and Sha Koon|}} As time passed by in the [[Coruscant Underworld/Legends|Coruscant Underworld]], Koon befriended a group of [[Zombie/Legends|zombie]]-like creatures called [[Cthon/Legends|Cthons]]. She plotted to cripple the [[Declaration of a New Order|newly crowned]] [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] Palpatine, the instigator of the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], by defeating his [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentice]], Darth Vader. Koon eventually contacted Palpatine over his secure [[Hologram/Legends|holographic]] channel and lied to the Emperor, expressing her desire to become his Sith apprentice by offering to eliminate Vader. Although Palpatine was not deceived by Koon, he sent Vader to the Jedi Temple underlevels to confront her. [[File:DeathOfShaKoon-STD.jpg|thumb|left|200px|Sha Koon is killed by Darth Vader.]] After Vader—who was forced to wear a [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|suit of life-preserving armor]] due to previous injuries [[Duel on Mustafar/Legends|dealt]] to him by Jedi Master Obi-Wan Kenobi—arrived in the underlevels, Koon was surprised to find that the person under Vader's mask was Anakin Skywalker. Vader recognized Koon's emotions and commented that he would have killed Koon if she had chosen not to flee during his raid on the Jedi Temple. Vader also stated that Palpatine saw through Koon's lies, claiming that Koon had only spoke via [[Holocomm/Legends|holocomm]] to hide her true intent of killing Palpatine. Koon revealed to Vader that her fallback plan was to cripple Palpatine by killing his Sith apprentice instead. With that, Koon unleashed the Cthons on Vader. As the creatures attacked the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]], Koon explained that they were easily [[Animal bond|controlled]] by the Force. The Cthons then threw an [[electroshock net]] on Vader, which damaged the [[Life support/Legends|life support]] systems on his armor. Vader broke free of the net and killed the Cthons, remarking that while the attempt was impressive, Koon had to face Vader then and there. The two combatants ignited their lightsabers, and Koon stated that she had been waiting to face him. Vader asserted that with her death, his mission to eliminate all the Jedi would be complete and added that she was the last Jedi alive to oppose the Sith. As Koon and Darth Vader began a [[Duel in the Jedi Temple Underlevels|lightsaber duel]], Koon contemplated the Sith Lord's claim that she was the last surviving Jedi. She became all the more determined to kill Vader, feeling that no one else would be able to do so if she failed. Koon was able to injure the Dark Lord by slashing him across his chest. Vader eventually gained the upper hand by [[Telekinesis/Legends|gripping]] her [[Throat/Legends|throat]] with the Force. He then drew Koon right towards [[Darth Vader's lightsaber/Legends|his lightsaber]], [[Shiak|impaling]] her through the chest. Before she died, Koon experienced a [[Force vision/Legends|vision]] through the Force of a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|rebellion]] against the Galactic Empire, Vader redeeming himself by [[Battle of Endor/Legends|killing]] Emperor Palpatine, and the [[New Jedi Order|rebirth]] of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] through Vader's children, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Realizing that the future did not depend on her, Koon died at peace. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Your emotions reveal you, ''Sha Koon''. Yes, I remember you from that day in the Archives. I would have killed you then, but you chose to flee. My Master bid me come here. You want to kill me and take my place. Or so you said. Your lie amused Lord Sidious. You spoke via holocomm to hide your true intent—to kill ''him''. Your plan is as pathetically simple as it is doomed."''
"''I have a fallback plan. ''Cripple'' him by ''killing'' you.|Darth Vader and Sha Koon|}} To the Kel Dor female Sha Koon, a telepath's images were more than words—they were bits of melody or explosions of feeling. She viewed her uncle's mind as rich and textured, yet full of contradictions. Yet, she knew that Plo Koon understood himself and what it meant to be a Jedi. After she rose to the rank of Jedi Knight, Koon was given a Padawan named Baylis Archan. Training Archan seemed natural to Koon, since Archan also possessed telepathic gifts. Koon viewed Archan's mind as playful, similar to a fast-moving [[Water/Legends|river]], and felt that Archan's thoughts were like [[Fish/Legends|fish]] in that river, darting and sparkling. [[File:PurgeSecondsToDieNJOVision.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Before her death at Darth Vader's hands, Sha Koon experienced a vision of Luke Skywalker's New Jedi Order.]] Koon believed that the Clone Wars were a violent and needless war. After her Padawan died in battle with the Separatists on Giju, she refused to return to combat against the Confederacy. When Darth Vader attacked the Jedi Temple, Koon chose to flee her attackers and was determined not to be killed like a mindless beast. Before Koon dueled Vader underneath the Jedi Temple, she told the Sith that she believed him to be a murderer of children, a traitor to his friends, and a destroyer of the Jedi to whom he was once allied. She expressed her desire to face Vader, and the Sith replied that Koon was the last Jedi and that his mission to eliminate every Jedi would be complete with her death. After hearing this, Koon felt that her success in killing Vader was of the utmost importance—as she believed that if she was unable to defeat Vader, no one else would be able to and the Sith would rule the galaxy. Before Koon died from the fatal injury dealt to her by Vader, she initially saw her death as the mark of her mission's failure. After she experienced a vision depicting the Emperor's death, Vader's redemption, and the Jedi Order's rebirth through Vader's children, she realized that the future of the galaxy did not depend on her and died at peace with herself. ==Powers and abilities== {{Quote|Corellian born, he too had a touch of telepathy and so my training him seemed natural.|Sha Koon, regarding Baylis Archan|}} [[File:VaderKoon4.jpg|thumb|left|250px|Koon duels Vader.]] Sha Koon possessed telepathic abilities and had a strong telepathic bond with her uncle, Plo Koon. When her uncle came to Dorin for her, she knew without a word being said that he had come to take her to Coruscant's Jedi Temple so that she could begin her training. Near the end of the Stark Hyperspace War, Koon was able to receive a telepathic contact from her uncle all the way from the planet Troiken while she herself was on Coruscant. When Order 66 was initiated, Koon sensed her uncle's death and temporarily slipped into unconsciousness. During the assault on the Jedi Temple led by Anakin Skywalker—who had taken up the mantle of Darth Vader—Koon was able to read Skywalker's thoughts and determine that he had joined the Sith. Before they dueled in the underlevels of the Jedi Temple, Koon used her telepathic abilities to confirm that Vader was Anakin Skywalker. Koon was able to utilize the ability to telekinetically push her enemies away with the Force and was capable of using the Force to [[Mind trick/Legends|control]] the Cthons, who were easily dominated with the Force. She was also skilled in the [[Lightsaber combat/Legends|use]] of a lightsaber and wounded Darth Vader before he gained the upper hand in their duel. Before she died, Koon had a vision through the Force that depicted future events. ==Equipment== Because Kel Dor were unable to breathe [[Oxygen/Legends|oxygen]],''[[The New Essential Guide to Alien Species]]'' Koon was forced to wear a set of protective goggles and an [[Antiox breath mask/Legends|antiox breath mask]] when not on her homeworld of Dorin. She also wore a set of [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi robes]] and used a lightsaber containing a [[Color/Legends|green]] [[Lightsaber crystal/Legends|crystal]]. ==Behind the scenes== Sha Koon was created by [[John Ostrander]] for the [[Star Wars (1998) 39|fourth issue]] of [[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War|''The Stark Hyperspace War'']] story arc of the ''[[Star Wars: Republic]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] series. Koon was later mentioned in [[StarWars.com]]'s [[Databank (original)|Databank]] entry on the planet Bal'demnic. In addition, she was mentioned in [[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] and ''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'' and received an entry in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]''. Koon was also the central Jedi protagonist in the comic [[Purge – Seconds to Die|''Star Wars: Purge: Seconds to Die'']], which detailed her death during the Great Jedi Purge. ==Appearances== *[[Star Wars (1998) 39|''Star Wars'' (1998) 39]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *[[Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures (video game)|''Star Wars: Clone Wars Adventures'']] {{Mo}} *''[[Purge – Seconds to Die]]'' ==Sources== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Sha Koon}} *''[[Star Wars: The Comics Companion]]'' {{C|Under the section ''The Stark Hyperspace War''}} *[[Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force (real-life book)|''Jedi vs. Sith: The Essential Guide to the Force'']] *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Clone Wars Campaign Guide]]'' {{Imo}} *{{InsiderCite|113|Blaster}} *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Comic 6.6|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' Comic 6.6]] *[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars Magazine 2|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars Magazine'' 2]] *{{DB|location|baldemnic|Bal'demnic}} *{{FactFile2014|12|46 BBY 1-4|The Stark Hyperspace War}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |cs=Sha Koon |de=Sha Koon |es=Sha Koon |fr=Sha Koon |nl=Sha Koon |pl=Sha Koon |pt=Sha Koon |fi=Sha Koon }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Koon, Sha}} [[Category:Females]] [[Category:Individuals with she/her pronouns]] [[Category:Jedi Purge victims]] [[Category:Kel Dor]] [[Category:Post-Ruusan Jedi Knights]]
